title: $:/plugins/rimir/appify
description: App-like layouts with view switching, statewrap integration, and a floating app launcher
name: appify
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings showcase changelog license
readme:
! Appify

Transform TiddlyWiki into an app platform. Define full-viewport applications with named layouts, statewrap-powered state channels, and a floating launcher to switch between apps and the default wiki.

!! Features

* ''App layouts'' — predefined grid templates (sidebar+main, topbar+sidebar+main, triple-column, dashboard, focus) with named view slots
* ''Statewrap integration'' — each app defines its own state channels and reactive rules
* ''View binding'' — each slot transcludes a tiddler that reads/writes channels by name
* ''Floating app switcher'' — always-visible FAB button to launch apps or return to the default wiki
* ''Edit mode modals'' — channel editor (add/rename/delete channels, set defaults), rule editor (add/delete reactive rules), debug inspector (live state, layout switcher)
* ''In-app tiddler editor'' — edit view tiddlers with the full TW EditTemplate (toolbar, tags, fields, type) in a draft-based overlay, with save/cancel
* ''Layout switching'' — switch layouts from the debug inspector; configs (view bindings, splits, proportions) are saved per layout and restored when switching back
* ''Conditional panes'' — split panes can have filter-based conditions; hidden panes collapse and siblings expand to fill the space
* ''Stacked views (tab groups)'' — a pane can hold multiple views with a tab bar; each view can have its own condition filter
* ''Draggable grid borders'' — resize grid tracks and split panes by dragging; handles respect grid-area spans
* ''Clone & delete'' — clone blueprint apps into your own namespace; clone individual views into your namespace; delete apps with full cleanup
* ''LLM integration'' — in-app chat panel (requires llm-connect) with context-scoped help pages (via llm-help). Activating an app automatically sets `rimir/appify` as the active help context; leaving returns to default. Deny-list protection hides tiddlers tagged `sensitive` from the LLM. Ships a `wikitext_render_in_app` tool that renders wikitext inside the active app's `<$statewrap>` scope, so the LLM can preview views with `statewrap-get` resolved against current channel values. The in-app chat's system prompt auto-injects the active app's layout, channels (with effective values), and view bindings (including conditional splits) so the LLM knows what is currently on screen
* ''Extensible'' — register apps by tagging tiddlers with `$:/tags/rimir/appify/app`; tag view tiddlers with `$:/tags/rimir/appify/view` for discovery

!! Quick start

# Install the plugin (requires statewrap)
# Open the sample app from the FAB (bottom-right)
# Enter edit mode (Ctrl+M) and click ''⧉ Clone'' to create your own copy
# Customize the cloned app — edit views, add channels, change layouts

The plugin ships sample apps as read-only blueprints. They must be cloned into your own namespace before use. Cloned apps and views live at `$:/config/rimir/appify/apps/` and `$:/config/rimir/appify/views/` by default (configurable via clone prefix setting).

!! App tiddler fields

|!Field |!Description |
|`appify-layout` |Layout template name (e.g., `topbar-sidebar-main`) |
|`appify-channels` |Space-separated channel names for statewrap |
|`appify-default-<name>` |Default value for channel `<name>` |
|`appify-view-<slot>` |Tiddler title to transclude into layout slot `<slot>` |
|`caption` |Display name in the app switcher |

The tiddler's text body contains `<$statewrap-rule>` definitions for reactive rules.

!! Edit mode (Ctrl+M)

In edit mode, all conditional panes are shown (maximal layout) regardless of their condition filters. The debug bar shows live channel values and five buttons:

* ''⚙ Channel editor'' — add, rename, delete channels and set default values
* ''⇄ Rule editor'' — view and edit reactive rules (when channel X changes → set channel Y)
* ''🔍 Debug inspector'' — app info, live state tiddler paths, layout switcher
* ''⧉ Clone'' — create a copy of the app and its views in a configurable namespace
* ''✕ Delete'' — permanently remove the app and all associated tiddlers

Each slot shows a label bar with: ''✎'' edit (opens modal editor), ''⧉'' clone (copies view to your namespace), ''↔'' / ''↕'' split buttons, and ''✕'' delete (for sub-slots). Below the label bar: a view selector dropdown (lists all tiddlers tagged `$:/tags/rimir/appify/view`), an inline condition editor (for sub-slots), and a wikitext textarea.

Stacked views show as functional tabs — click a tab to see its editor. The label bar pencil opens the modal for the currently active tab.

!! Built-in layouts

|!Name |!Slots |!Description |
|`sidebar-main` |sidebar, main |Left sidebar + main content |
|`topbar-sidebar-main` |topbar, sidebar, main |Top bar + left sidebar + main content |
|`triple-column` |left, center, right |Three equal columns |
|`dashboard` |topbar, left, right |Top bar + two equal panels |
|`focus` |main |Single full-viewport pane |

Layout configs (view bindings, splits, proportions) are saved per layout and automatically restored when switching back.

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0 or later
* statewrap plugin

title: $:/plugins/rimir/capture
description: Stream-based inbox for quick capture with triage to typed entities
name: capture
author: rimir
readme:
! Capture

A generic inbox plugin for quick idea capture using streams. Throw in thoughts, ideas, and fleeting notes — then triage them into structured typed entities when ready.

!! Features

* **Inbox stream** — rapid keyboard-driven capture via the streams plugin
* **Triage view** — review captures, promote to typed entities (task, meeting, etc.)
* **Configurable** — set which stream tiddler serves as inbox
* **Generic** — works with any typed model, not tied to a specific domain

!! Prerequisites

* [[Streams plugin|$:/plugins/sq/streams]] for stream-based note entry
* [[Typed plugin|$:/plugins/rimir/typed]] for promoting captures to structured entities

!! Quick start

# Open the inbox view and start typing — entries are saved as stream nodes
# When ready to triage, switch to the triage view
# Click "Promote" on a capture to open the typed spawner with text pre-filled

title: $:/plugins/rimir/components
description: Reusable UI component framework with composable mixins and facade components
name: components
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings showcase changelog license
readme:
! components

Reusable UI component framework with composable mixins and facade components. Build complex interactive widgets by combining small, focused building blocks.

!! Key features

* ''Facade components'' — ready-to-use `pills` (with inline/popup filtering) and `vtabs` components
* ''Mixin system'' — composable behaviours: state handling, single/multi select, delegation
* ''Style-by-type'' — swappable visual styles (e.g., `simple-hoverable-pill`, `tabs-style`)
* ''Menu component'' — configurable menu with delegate-based actions
* ''Showcase'' — live interactive examples demonstrating all components

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Use facade components directly via transclusion. See the showcase tiddler (`$:/plugins/rimir/components/showcase`) for interactive examples of all available components and mixins.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/core-hook
name: core hook
description: Unified settings hub for rimir plugins in the ControlPanel
author: rimir
readme:
! core hook

Unified settings hub for all rimir plugins. Adds a "Rimi Plugins" tab to ControlPanel > Settings with a vertical sidebar listing all installed rimir plugins and their settings.

!! Key features

* ''Dynamic discovery'' — automatically finds all installed `$:/plugins/rimir/*` plugins
* ''Vertical sidebar'' — plugin list with icons, names, and version badges
* ''Auto-discovery of settings'' — transcludes each plugin's `settings` tiddler as content panel
* ''Fallback panel'' — plugins without settings show an info card with link to documentation

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Install plugin. All rimir plugins with a `settings` tiddler automatically appear in ControlPanel > Settings > Rimi Plugins.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/cytoscape-engine
description: Cytoscape.js graph engine adapter for tw-graph with compound node support
name: cytoscape engine
author: rimir
readme:
! Cytoscape Engine

An alternative graph rendering engine for [[tw-graph|$:/plugins/flibbles/graph]] powered by [[Cytoscape.js|https://js.cytoscape.org/]] (v3.33.2).

!! Key Features

* ''Drop-in replacement'' for vis-network — existing `<$graph>` markup works unchanged
* ''Interactive clusters'' — drag nodes onto each other to group, double-click to rename/color, drag away to remove
* ''Per-graph cluster config'' — each graph stores clusters in its own config tiddler
* ''Nested clusters'' — clusters inside clusters
* ''COSE layout'' — Compound Spring Embedder that handles nested groups natively
* Full event support: hover, click, drag, double-click
* Position persistence and physics simulation

!! Quick Start

# Install this plugin alongside `flibbles/graph`
# Go to ''Settings > Graph Engine'' and select ''Cytoscape''
# All existing graphs render with Cytoscape.js

!! Interactive Clusters

Enable with `clusterData` graph property pointing to a config tiddler:

```
<$graph>
<$properties $for="graph" clusterData="$:/config/my-clusters">
  <$node $tiddler="Alice" label="Alice"/>
  <$node $tiddler="Bob" label="Bob"/>
</$properties>
</$graph>
```

|!Gesture |!Effect |
|Drag node onto node |Creates cluster containing both |
|Drag node onto cluster |Adds to cluster |
|Drag 200+ px from grab point |Removes from cluster |
|Double-click cluster label |Rename + color picker |

!! Prerequisites

* `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph` (tw-graph) v1.6.0+
* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0+

title: $:/plugins/rimir/diffsync
description: Field-level tiddler comparison with selective sync
name: diffsync
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings panel review changelog license
readme:
! diffsync -- field-level tiddler comparison with selective sync

Compare two tiddlers field by field. Single-line fields show source vs target values. Multiline fields (like `text`) are broken into line-based hunks with surrounding context, so you can see exactly what changed. Toggle individual hunks or fields to pick which version to keep, then apply in either direction.

!! Key features

* ''Field-level comparison'' -- every field is compared independently, skipping `title`
* ''Line-based hunks'' -- multiline fields use diff-match-patch to produce line-level diffs grouped into hunks with configurable context lines
* ''Per-hunk selective sync'' -- toggle each hunk between source and target before applying
* ''Bidirectional apply'' -- apply selected changes to the target tiddler or back to the source
* ''Review mode'' -- accept or skip individual changes; starts with all changes skipped so you selectively accept what you want
* ''Auto-refresh'' -- diff view updates live when source or target tiddler is modified
* ''Swap and re-compare'' -- swap source/target with one click, auto-re-compares if a result exists
* ''Clean state management'' -- all UI state lives in `$:/state/diffsync/*` and `$:/temp/diffsync/*`, cleared on new comparisons

!! Quick start

# Open the diffsync panel (plugin tab or drag `$:/plugins/rimir/diffsync/panel` into your story)
# Select a source and target tiddler from the dropdowns
# Click ''Compare'' to see field-by-field differences
# For single-line fields, toggle between source and target values
# For multiline fields, toggle individual hunks
# Click ''Apply to target'' or ''Apply to source'' to write the selected changes

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0+
* Theme plugin (`$:/plugins/rimir/theme`) for `rr-` utility classes

title: $:/plugins/rimir/doc-template
name: doc template
description: Branded documentation system for rimir plugins
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings hub changelog license
readme:
! doc template

Branded documentation system for all rimir plugins. Renders plugin documentation with a consistent layout featuring a dark header, tab navigation, breadcrumbs, and an auto-discovery hub that lists all documented plugins.

!! Key features

* ''Auto-discovery hub'' — card grid grouped by category, finds all documented plugins automatically
* ''Branded page layout'' — header, breadcrumb, tabs, and footer for consistent presentation
* ''Field-based discovery'' — no tags needed, uses `rdt.*` fields for metadata
* ''Callout boxes'' — four types: info, warning, tip, danger
* ''In-place navigation'' — seamless browsing when accessed from the hub
* ''Related plugins footer'' — links to related plugins at the bottom of each page

!! Prerequisites

* theme plugin (for CSS classes)

!! Quick start

Create `doc-overview.tid` in your plugin with `rdt.plugin-key`, `rdt.section`, `rdt.plugin-name`, `rdt.plugin-subtitle`, `rdt.hub-category` fields. The version is read automatically from `plugin.info`. Transclude `$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page` and fill the `body` slot. The hub auto-discovers it.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/explide
description: Interactive testcase composition IDE
author: rimir
name: explide
list: readme documentation settings examples changelog license
readme:
! explide

Interactive testcase composition IDE for TiddlyWiki. Create config tiddlers that assemble plugins, shadow tiddlers, and ad-hoc content into isolated `$testcase` sandboxes — all from within the wiki.

!! Key features

* ''Config-driven'' — each config tiddler (`rri.type: explide/config`) defines a complete testcase sandbox
* ''Plugin import'' — import entire plugins or individual shadow tiddlers, with bulk import from filter
* ''Explide data'' — override imported shadow tiddlers with editable copies using `$rri$:` field remapping
* ''Field editor'' — add, edit, and delete `$rri$:` remapped fields via the FIELDS tab
* ''Ad-hoc tiddlers'' — create text-only tiddlers inline without separate files
* ''Snapshot & diff'' — take snapshots of explide data, compare changes, and restore previous states
* ''Diagnostics'' — detect missing plugins, unused imports, and stale snapshots
* ''Layout toggle'' — switch between stacked and side-by-side view
* ''Output tiddler'' — configurable which tiddler the testcase Output tab displays (defaults to `Output`)
* ''ViewTemplate integration'' — config tiddlers automatically render the IDE when viewed

!! Prerequisites

* [[$:/plugins/rimir/theme]] — CSS utility classes (`rr-*`)
* [[$:/plugins/rimir/components]] — tabs and UI components
* [[$:/plugins/kookma/shiraz]] — slider macro and UI utilities

!! Quick start

# Create a new tiddler
# Set the field `rri.type` to `explide/config`
# Save — the IDE appears automatically in the tiddler view
# Use the CONFIG tab to import plugins and select shadow tiddlers
# Create an ad-hoc tiddler named `Output` in the ADHOC tab — this is what the testcase renders
# Click on explide data tabs to edit overridden tiddlers in EDITOR, manage fields in FIELDS, or compare in DIFF

!! Bundled examples

* `$:/plugins/rimir/explide/examples/callout-sandbox` — theme callout types
* `$:/plugins/rimir/explide/examples/mini-app` — counter app with state management
* `$:/plugins/rimir/explide/examples/component-lab` — pills facade components

title: $:/plugins/rimir/ext-connect
description: HTTP endpoint for external tools — inbound (push tiddlers) and outbound (text-command extraction)
name: ext connect
author: rimir
readme:
! ext connect

Push tiddlers into the wiki from external tools via CLI import — no running server needed. A shared resolution pipeline with profile-based templates, context-aware rules, and post-creation actions transforms raw input into fully-formed tiddlers.

!! Key features

* ''CLI import'' — primary inbound path via `--import` deserializer, no server required
* ''Context resolution'' — templates reference context tiddlers for smart field population and rule matching
* ''Profile-based templates'' — map profiles to template tiddlers with filter-based field resolution
* ''Rule-based dispatch'' — ordered rules use TW filters to match context and select templates
* ''Post-creation actions'' — template `text` field runs action widgets server-side after tiddler creation
* ''Text-command extraction'' — outbound processing of tiddler content for external tool integration

!! Prerequisites

* Set `TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE=1` environment variable for CLI import

!! Quick start

Push a tiddler via CLI:

```
TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE=1 npx tiddlywiki mywiki --import input.json application/x-rimir-ext-connect
```

Where `input.json` contains: `{"text": "Hello", "title": "MyTiddler"}`

''Fallback: POST API'' (may be deprecated) — if a server is running, you can also use `POST /api/ext-connect/put-tiddler` with `X-Requested-With: TiddlyWiki` header. See the reference docs for details.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help
description: llm-help pages for third-party TiddlyWiki plugins — host plugin that contributes browsable, context-scoped documentation under the plugins/* path
author: rimir
name: external llm help
readme:
! External LLM Help

llm-help pages for third-party TiddlyWiki plugins. This plugin contributes a `plugins/<plugin-slug>/...` subtree to the [[llm-help|$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] browser so an LLM working in a tiddler can discover and read context-aware documentation for the wiki's installed plugins.

!! Why a separate plugin?

* Third-party plugins (`flibbles/graph`, `evidentlyCube/AutoComplete`, ...) cannot be modified to ship their own llm-help pages.
* Centralising their documentation here keeps it versioned, distributable, and discoverable through the same `llm_help` MCP tool the user already trusts.
* Each documented plugin gets its own subfolder under `pages/` so adding a new one is a self-contained change.

!! Layout

```
pages/
  plugins.tid                    -- root listing of documented plugins
  tw5-graph/
    root.tid                     -- llm-help-path: plugins/tw5-graph
    overview.tid                 -- plugins/tw5-graph/overview
    concepts.tid
    concepts/<thing>.tid
    widgets.tid
    widgets/<widget>.tid
    filters.tid
    templates.tid
    recipes.tid
    recipes/<task>.tid
    gotchas.tid
  <next-plugin>/
    ...
```

Every page is tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` and gated by `llm-help-context: plugins/<plugin-slug>` so the entire subtree is opt-in.

!! For maintainers

Use the project skill ``/tw-derive-llmhelp-from-plugin <plugin-id>`` to generate or refresh a plugin's page tree. The skill reads TiddlyWiki gotcha memories first, analyses the plugin source, proposes a tree, and writes the pages.

!! Requirements

* [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] -- the help browser and `llm_help` tool.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/file-pipeline
description: Configurable binary data processing pipelines — thumbnails, text extraction, format conversion, and LLM analysis
name: file pipeline
author: rimir
readme:
! File Pipeline

Configurable binary data processing pipelines for TiddlyWiki. Define multi-step workflows that transform uploaded files into thumbnails, extracted text, format conversions, and LLM-generated analysis — all linked to the source file with automatic lifecycle management.

!! Key Features

* ''Configurable pipelines'' — define processing steps per file type as tiddlers (no restart needed)
* ''Command steps'' — run shell commands (ImageMagick, pdftotext, pandoc, pdfimages, custom scripts)
* ''LLM steps'' — send extracted text to an LLM for summarization, annotation, or analysis
* ''Step chaining'' — output of one step feeds into the next (e.g., DOCX → PDF → thumbnail)
* ''Artifact lifecycle'' — all generated tiddlers are linked via `_artifact_source` for automatic cascade delete/rename
* ''Auto-matching'' — pipelines auto-select based on MIME type, or can be explicitly chosen
* ''Interactive mode'' — LLM steps can pause for human review before creating artifacts

!! Default Pipelines

|!Pipeline |!File Types |!Steps |
|pdf |PDF |Thumbnail + text extraction + image extraction |
|docx |DOCX |Convert to PDF + thumbnail + text extraction |
|image |JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP |Thumbnail |
|xlsx |XLSX |Text extraction |

!! Prerequisites

* ''runner'' plugin — provides `runner-actions.json` command execution
* ''file-upload'' plugin — provides URI resolution and artifact cascade
* ''llm-connect'' plugin — required only for LLM pipeline steps
* External tools: `magick` (thumbnails), `pdftotext` (PDF text), `pandoc` (DOCX/markdown), `pdfimages` (PDF images), `libreoffice` (DOC→DOCX, DOCX→PDF)

!! LLM Step Configuration

LLM steps can be configured at three levels (highest priority first):

# ''Step level'' — `systemPrompt`, `provider`, `model` fields in the pipeline step JSON
# ''Dropzone level'' — `prop-system-prompt`, `prop-provider`, `prop-model` attributes on `<$file-dropzone>`
# ''Global default'' — llm-connect's provider/model/system-prompt settings

```
<$file-dropzone pipeline="docx-llm"
  prop-system-prompt="$:/my/custom-system-prompt"
  prop-model="gpt-4o">
```

Step-level fields support `{{config:$:/config/...}}` indirection to reference config tiddlers.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/file-upload
description: Upload files to configurable locations with rename, delete, thumbnails, artifact cascade, and deduplication
name: file upload
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings test changelog license
readme:
! file upload

Upload media files to configurable storage locations with automatic tiddler creation, rename, delete, thumbnail generation, artifact lifecycle management, and content-hash deduplication.

!! Key features

* ''Multi-location support'' — upload files to any registered writable location, not just `/files/`. Create and manage locations via the settings UI.
* ''Location registry'' — tag-based tiddler convention (`$:/tags/rimir/file-upload/location`) for defining file storage locations with URI prefix, base path, and writable flag
* ''Drag-and-drop upload'' — `<$file-dropzone>` widget with `location` attribute to target any writable location
* ''Thumbnail generation'' — automatic thumbnails for images and PDFs via ImageMagick (configurable resolution)
* ''Thumbnail rendering'' — show thumbnails by default with click-to-expand and toolbar toggle
* ''File rename'' — renaming a tiddler renames the physical file on writable locations, including `_generated/` thumbnails
* ''File delete'' — deleting a tiddler offers to delete the file on writable locations, including thumbnails
* ''Artifact cascade'' — deleting or renaming a file tiddler automatically cascades to all derived artifacts (extractions, summaries, etc.) via `_artifact_source` field
* ''EXIF extraction'' — automatically extract metadata (date, camera, GPS, dimensions, exposure) from uploaded images and map to tiddler fields. Configurable field mapping per location.
* ''Deduplication'' — optional global content-hash dedup skips identical uploads

!! Optional integration

* ''scattered-binaries'' — when installed, its discovered locations automatically register with the location registry, enabling artifact cascade for scattered files
* ''llm-connect'' — when installed, derived artifacts (`.extracted` tiddlers, summaries) are tagged with `_artifact_source` for automatic cascade cleanup

!! Prerequisites

* ImageMagick — required for thumbnail generation and EXIF extraction (`magick` on PATH)
* Ghostscript — additionally required for PDF thumbnails (`gswin64c` on Windows, `gs` on Linux)

!! Quick start

Drop files onto any `<$file-dropzone>` widget. Configure locations, thumbnail settings, and allowed media types in ControlPanel > Settings > file upload.

```
<$file-dropzone actions="""
<$action-createtiddler $basetitle=<<filename>>
  _canonical_uri=<<canonical-uri>>
  _thumbnail_uri=<<generated-uri>>
  type=<<type>>/>
""">
Drop files here
</$file-dropzone>
```

Upload to a specific location:

```
<$file-dropzone location="my-assets" actions="...">
Drop files into my-assets
</$file-dropzone>
```

title: $:/plugins/rimir/filesystem-watcher
description: Watches the tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes and syncs them into the wiki
name: filesystem watcher
author: rimir
readme:
! filesystem watcher

Watches the tiddlers directory for external filesystem changes (created, modified, deleted by tools like Claude Code) and syncs them into the running wiki in real time.

!! Key features

* ''Live file sync'' — detects file changes via chokidar and updates the wiki store automatically
* ''Reload alerts'' — yellow alert + red refresh icon for changes requiring a browser reload (module tiddlers, system tiddlers)
* ''Loop prevention'' — hooks TW's save/delete functions to ignore its own filesystem writes
* ''CLI mode skip'' — exits immediately when `TIDDLYWIKI_CLI_MODE=1` so CLI imports don't hang
* ''Ignored tiddlers'' — skips noisy tiddlers like `$:/StoryList` that change frequently and have no value for external sync
* ''Configurable poll interval'' — bundled default `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` (override in wiki store to change)

!! Prerequisites

* chokidar (npm dependency, installed with the wiki)

!! Quick start

Install plugin and `chokidar` npm package. External file changes in the tiddlers directory sync automatically into the running wiki. The poll interval is set via `$:/config/SyncPollingInterval` (default: {{$:/config/SyncPollingInterval}}ms).

title: $:/plugins/rimir/frontmatter
description: Embed tiddler fields as YAML frontmatter in markdown files
name: frontmatter
author: rimir
readme:
! Frontmatter

Store tiddler metadata as YAML frontmatter directly inside `.md` files — no `.meta` sidecar needed. Any `.md` file with YAML frontmatter is automatically detected and re-parsed at boot.

!! Features

* ''Auto-detection'' — any `.md` file with `---` delimited YAML frontmatter is recognized automatically
* ''Title derivation'' — frontmatter `title` field > existing clean title > derived from filepath
* ''Syncer-safe'' — change events suppressed during re-parse to prevent file corruption
* ''Two-pass re-parse'' — handles both boot-loaded files and `tiddlywiki.files` entries
* ''YAML serialization'' — tags and list fields serialized as YAML arrays
* ''Orphan cleanup'' — automatic removal of orphaned `.meta` files when migrating
* ''Filesystem-watcher compatible'' — live external edits work seamlessly

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0+ (Node.js server edition)
* The official `tiddlywiki/markdown` plugin (for rendering)

!! Quick Start

# Set a tiddler's type to `text/x-frontmattered-markdown`
# The tiddler is saved as a `.md` file with YAML frontmatter on disk
# Or place any `.md` file with YAML frontmatter in your tiddlers directory — it is auto-detected at boot

title: $:/plugins/rimir/git-int
description: Git version control integration for the TiddlyWiki editor
name: git integration
author: rimir
readme:
! git integration

Git version control for individual tiddlers from within the TiddlyWiki editor. Commit, diff, restore, and stash changes without leaving edit mode.

!! Key features

* ''Inline diff'' — view current changes vs. last commit side by side
* ''Commit'' — commit individual tiddlers with a commit message
* ''Stash'' — stash/unstash and inspect stashed changes
* ''Restore'' — restore tiddler content to any previous version
* ''Draft-aware'' — works with unsaved edits (browser sends draft fields to server)
* ''Multi-repo'' — automatically detects which git repo each tiddler belongs to (supports nested repos)
* ''Toolbar button'' — accessible from the edit toolbar

!! Prerequisites

* Git installed and available on the server's PATH
* Tiddlers directory (or subdirectories) must be inside a git repository

!! Quick start

Initialize git in your tiddlers directory (or use separate repos for subdirectories like `tiddlers/work/` and `tiddlers/private/`). Open any tiddler in edit mode and click the git icon in the toolbar to access diff, commit, and stash operations.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl
name: json dsl
description: Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki
author: rimir
readme:
! json dsl

Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki. Compose JSON structures using widget syntax instead of string manipulation.

!! Key features

* ''Composable widget set'' — `<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`, `<$json-prop>`, `<$json-val>`, `<$json-item>`
* ''Output widgets'' — emit to tiddler field (`<$json-save>`) or action variable (`<$json-emit>`)
* ''Debug widget'' — `<$json-debug>` for inspecting output during development
* ''Widget tree integration'' — seamless integration with TiddlyWiki's widget tree and filter expressions

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Build JSON declaratively: `<$json-obj><$json-prop key="name" value="hello"/><$json-emit/></$json-obj>`.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/llm-connect
description: In-browser LLM integration with tool use — connect TiddlyWiki to Claude, OpenAI, Azure, and other LLM APIs
author: rimir
name: llm connect
readme:
! llm connect

In-browser LLM integration with tool use. Connect TiddlyWiki to Claude, OpenAI, Azure, and other LLM APIs — the wiki acts as the orchestrator: sends prompts, receives tool calls, executes them against `$tw.wiki`, and returns results. No server required.

!! Key features

* ''Two modes'' — interactive chat (multi-turn, tool use) and one-shot actions (context template → LLM → output target)
* ''Pair editing'' — toolbar button opens an embedded chat panel on any tiddler for collaborative editing with the LLM
* ''Provider adapters'' — Claude, OpenAI, and Azure OpenAI out of the box, extensible for other providers
* ''Dynamic model selection'' — fetch available models from provider APIs; per-chat model selector with provider locking
* ''Tool system'' — built-in tools for searching, reading, navigating, and modifying tiddlers; user-defined tools via wikitext. `create_tiddler` supports `basetitle` for auto-numbered titles
* ''Per-chat tool selection'' — wrench icon opens a dropdown to toggle individual tools per chat; tool groups as quick presets
* ''Help context selector'' — question mark icon (requires llm-help plugin) opens a dropdown to toggle llm-help context keys, with All/None quick buttons and configurable context groups
* ''Multimodal context'' — attach PDFs, images, and text tiddlers to conversations via a context filter; binary files are fetched, base64-encoded, and sent inline to the LLM
* ''Document extraction'' — PDF/DOCX/XLSX auto-converted to markdown text via pdftotext/pandoc/openpyxl before sending to the LLM. Cached per document. Falls back to base64 when runner is unavailable
* ''Model tier routing'' — assign cost tiers (cheap/default/expensive) to tools; orchestrator auto-switches models per tool round. Toggle via layers icon in chat header
* ''Context templates'' — wikitext templates that render input for the LLM from one or more source tiddlers
* ''Output targets'' — write LLM responses to fields, new tiddlers, or just display in chat
* ''Debug panel'' — toggle a live preview of the request payload or inspect the last sent request; base64 data abbreviated for readability
* ''Resizable chat'' — drag the chat panel edges or the chat history resize handle to adjust layout
* ''Markdown rendering'' — assistant messages are rendered as formatted markdown (tables, code blocks, lists, etc.) using the TiddlyWiki markdown plugin
* ''Collapsible tool messages'' — tool call and result messages are collapsed by default; click to expand individually or use the ⚙️ toggle for all
* ''Tiddler protection'' — allow/deny modes with restricted wiki proxy. Base protection filter (always enforced, both modes) + hard protection filter (final safety net after all user input) ensure `tag[sensitive]` tiddlers are never accessible. Per-chat mode and filter overrides via shield icon; switching modes auto-negates the filter. Widget-level `allowFilter`/`denyFilter`/`protectionMode` attributes for recipe panels
* ''Plugin visibility'' — tick plugins in settings to exclude their shadow tiddlers from LLM access (all readable by default)
* ''Accessible tiddlers panel'' — eye icon (👁️) shows a live list of all tiddlers (including shadows) the LLM can currently access based on the active protection filter. Drop tiddlers onto the panel to grant access (adds to whitelist in allow mode, exempts from blacklist in deny mode)
* ''Chat export/import'' — arrow buttons in the chat header export the entire conversation as JSON to the clipboard, or import a previously exported chat from the clipboard (falls back to a paste dialog if clipboard read access is denied). All state is preserved: messages, provider, model, tool selection, protection filters, and context
* ''Chat pin/save/restore'' — 📌 icon pins a chat to persist it to disk. Pinned chats auto-sync all state on every change. Restore saved chats after browser reload or server restart. One save slot per widget context (global chat, chat view, each pair-edit tiddler)
* ''Audit logging'' — write operations (set-field, create, delete) execute immediately with an audit log

!! Prerequisites

* An API key for at least one supported provider (Claude, OpenAI, or Azure OpenAI)
* A modern browser (async/await support)

!! Quick start

# Install the plugin and configure your API key in Settings
# Open the chat from the page toolbar button
# Ask a question — the LLM can search and read your wiki via tools
# Click the pair-edit button in any tiddler's toolbar to start a collaborative editing session

For one-shot actions, add a button to any tiddler:

```
<$button>
  Summarize
  <$llm-action
    tiddler=<<currentTiddler>>
    prompt="Summarize the most important points in 3 bullets."
  />
</$button>
```

title: $:/plugins/rimir/llm-help
description: Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions — lets LLMs browse context-aware documentation to solve wiki tasks autonomously
author: rimir
name: llm help
readme:
! LLM Help

A self-discoverable, hierarchical help system that lets LLMs browse context-aware documentation to solve wiki-specific tasks autonomously.

!! How it works

The plugin provides a single MCP tool `llm_help` that LLMs call to navigate a tree of help pages:

* `llm_help()` -- returns top-level help categories
* `llm_help("data-model")` -- navigates deeper into a category
* `llm_help("data-model/person/create")` -- reaches a specific help page

Each level returns child topics with descriptions, so the LLM decides where to go based on its current task. Help pages can contain dynamic content -- rendered at call time from the wiki's live state (e.g., typed data schemas, existing instances).

!! Key features

* ''Hierarchical navigation'' -- help pages organized in a tree of any depth
* ''Dynamic content'' -- pages rendered as wikitext at call time, pulling live data from the wiki
* ''Context-aware'' -- pages can be scoped to specific contexts (e.g., an appify app)
* ''Multi-path mounting'' -- same page reachable from multiple paths in the tree
* ''Plugin-extensible'' -- any plugin can contribute help pages via tagged tiddlers
* ''User shortcuts'' -- users can add quick-reference help pages via the settings UI
* ''Context selector'' -- settings popup to toggle active context keys with page counts
* ''Help browser'' -- settings tab lets users see and navigate what the LLM sees

!! For plugin authors

To add help pages from your plugin, create shadow tiddlers with:

* Tag: `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page`
* Field `llm-help-path`: navigation path (space-separated for multiple paths)
* Field `llm-help-description`: one-line description shown in parent listings
* Optional `llm-help-sort`: numeric sort order within siblings
* Optional `llm-help-context`: space-separated context keys (page hidden when context doesn't match)

Pages with no `llm-help-context` are always visible. Pages with a context are only visible when the active context matches.

!! Requirements

* [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-connect]] -- provides the MCP tool infrastructure
* [[$:/plugins/rimir/theme]] -- provides UI styling classes

title: $:/plugins/rimir/minver
description: Browser-side tiddler versioning with snapshots
name: minver
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings manager groups changelog license
readme:
! minver — browser-side tiddler versioning

Keep snapshot versions of your tiddlers directly in the browser. Snapshots are stored compressed in localStorage and survive page reloads.

!! Key features

* ''Manual snapshots'' — take a snapshot anytime from the editor preview pane
* ''Auto-snapshots'' — automatically captures the previous version on every save
* ''Auto-snapshots on create/delete'' — automatically captures initial state on creation and final state on deletion
* ''Diff view'' — per-field comparison with `<$diff-text>`, integrated as an editor preview type
* ''Field-level rollback'' — revert individual fields or the entire tiddler
* ''Manager revert'' — revert any snapshot directly from the manager
* ''Change-groups'' — record a session of changes (creates, edits, deletes) and review/rollback them as a unit
* ''Compressed storage'' — uses native CompressionStream to minimize localStorage usage
* ''Export/Import'' — transfer snapshots between wikis via a single JSON tiddler

!! Quick start

!!! Snapshots

# Open any tiddler for editing
# Click the eye icon in the editor toolbar and select "snapshots"
# Click "Take snapshot" to create a manual snapshot
# Edit the tiddler, then select your snapshot to see the diff
# Use the undo button next to any field to revert it

!!! Change-groups

# Click the record button (●) in the page toolbar to start recording changes
# Make your edits (create, modify, delete tiddlers)
# Click stop (■) to finish — view your change-group in the "groups" plugin tab
# Use "Rollback" to undo all changes in the group

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0+
* Theme plugin (`$:/plugins/rimir/theme`)
* A modern browser with CompressionStream support (Chrome 80+, Firefox 113+, Safari 16.4+)

title: $:/plugins/rimir/namespace
description: Namespace-aware link resolution with walk-up from title prefixes
name: namespace
author: rimir
readme:
! Namespace

Namespace-aware link resolution for TiddlyWiki. Replaces the core
{{{ [[…]] }}} parser rule so link targets are resolved relative to the
source tiddler's own title prefix — with declarative overrides and
pluggable pseudo-segments.

The plugin is ''explicit-first'': advanced features (walk-up, aliases,
pseudo-expansion, implicit context) are all off by default and can be
toggled individually in the [[settings tab|$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/settings]].
The core mechanism — literal match + `\context` pragma / `<$context>` widget +
mount points — is always active.

→ ''See the [[examples tab|$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/examples]]''
for live, in-plugin demos of every feature described below.

!! Resolution pipeline

A link {{{ [[REF]] }}} is resolved in this order. Stages marked ☐ are
off by default and activated via feature flags in settings.

# ''Literal on raw REF'' — a real tiddler always beats an alias or
  pseudo expansion. Avoids silent redirection if a tiddler and an alias
  share a name.
# ☐ ''Alias rewrite'' — exact aliases (tiddlers tagged
  `$:/tags/NamespaceAlias` with `short` + `expands-to` fields) and
  pattern aliases (`$:/tags/NamespacePatternAlias`, `pattern` +
  `replacement`). Chained up to 3 hops for cycle safety.
# ''Mount rewrite'' — `$:/tags/NamespaceMount` with `from` / `to`.
  Longest `from` wins; one-pass (no cascading).
# ☐ ''Pseudo-segment expansion'' — any segment starting with `_` is
  looked up in the pseudo-registry. Matching resolvers receive
  `(prefix, wiki)` and return a replacement segment or null.
# ''Literal on expanded REF'' — catches the aliased, mounted, or
  pseudo-expanded forms.
# ''Absolute'' — if REF contains a slash at this point, it's looked up
  literally only (no walk-up, no context prefix).
# ''Context prefix'' — if a context is declared (see below), try
  `<context>/REF`. When pseudo-expansion is enabled, pseudo-segments
  inside the context are also expanded.
# ☐ ''Walk-up'' — walk up the source tiddler's title prefix:
  from //source// `a/b/c/d/X`, try `a/b/c/d/REF`, `a/b/c/REF`,
  `a/b/REF`, `a/REF`, `REF`. First hit wins.
# ''Unresolved'' — rendered with the `ns-unresolved` class
  (wavy underline + ⚠ marker).

!! Feature flags

Four optional features can be toggled in the
[[settings tab|$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/settings]]. All are ''off'' by
default. No server restart required — toggling a flag takes effect
immediately.

|!Flag |!What it enables |
|Walk-up |Stage 8: path-based walk-up from source tiddler |
|Implicit context |`context:` field fallback (in addition to `\context` pragma / `<$context>` widget) |
|Pseudo expansion |`_latest` and other `_foo` pseudo-segment modules |
|Aliases |Exact + pattern alias resolution |

!! Declaring a context

Two always-on mechanisms, plus an optional field-based fallback:

* ''Pragma at the top of a tiddler'' (always active):

```
\context OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3

now [[V3.3]] resolves to OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3/V3.3
```

* ''Widget for a scoped region'' (always active):

```
<$context prefix="OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3">
  [[V3.3]] resolves here
</$context>
regular rendering resumes outside
```

* ''Field on the tiddler'' (requires ''implicit context'' flag):

```
title: my-notes
context: OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3
...
```

Priority: widget variable (from pragma or `<$context>`) wins over field.
That makes `\context` an effective "pin" — dropping `\context OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3`
at the top of a doc overrides any field-based context and freezes the
doc's resolutions against version 4.0.3.

!! Aliases — short tokens for common refs

Create a tiddler tagged `$:/tags/NamespaceAlias`:

```
short: OWASP_V3.3
expands-to: OWASP/ASVS/_latest/V3.3
```

Now {{{ [[OWASP_V3.3]] }}} from anywhere resolves via the alias +
`_latest` expansion.

For many similar refs, use a pattern alias (tag
`$:/tags/NamespacePatternAlias`):

```
pattern: ^OWASP_V(.+)$
replacement: OWASP/ASVS/_latest/V$1
```

Now `OWASP_V3.3`, `OWASP_V4.2`, `OWASP_V5.0`, … all resolve.

Exact beats pattern. See the [[examples tab|$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/examples/aliases]]
for live demos.

!! Mount points — short prefixes for deep subtrees

For a whole subtree rather than a single token, use a mount
(tag `$:/tags/NamespaceMount`):

```
from: docs
to: project-x/documentation/public
```

Now `[[docs/intro]]`, `[[docs/api/v2]]`, etc. rewrite to
`project-x/documentation/public/intro`, `…/api/v2`, and so on. Longest
`from` wins when multiple mounts could match. See the
[[Mount points example|$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/examples/mounts]].

!! Built-in pseudo-segments

* ''`_latest`'' — resolves to the highest-version immediate child of its
  prefix. A "version" is plain dotted-numeric with optional `-prerelease`
  (`1`, `3.0`, `4.0.3`, `1.0-beta`, `2.0.0-rc.1`). Comparison is numeric
  per segment, with releases sorting above their prereleases.

!! Examples

''Walk-up from a deep title.'' A tiddler titled
`a/b/OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3/docs/intro` can write {{{ [[V3.3]] }}} and it
resolves to `a/b/OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3/V3.3`.

''`_latest` at a fixed anchor.'' A reference
{{{ [[OWASP/ASVS/_latest/V3.3]] }}} resolves to
`OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3/V3.3` when `4.0.3` is the highest version child under
`OWASP/ASVS/`. Add `5.0` and the same reference re-points.

''Context + bare ref.'' From any tiddler:

```
\context OWASP/ASVS/_latest
[[V3.3]]   ← resolves via context → OWASP/ASVS/<max>/V3.3
[[V4.2]]   ← same, but if missing in <max>, link is unresolved
```

!! Adding your own pseudo-segment

Pseudo-segments are pluggable. Drop a JS module alongside or in a
dependent plugin, declaring `module-type: rimir-ns-pseudo`:

```
/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourspace/yourname/pseudo/_first.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: rimir-ns-pseudo
\*/

var util = require("$:/plugins/rimir/namespace/resolver.js");

exports.name = "_first";

exports.resolve = function(prefix, wiki) {
    if(!prefix) { return null; }
    var kids = util.listImmediateChildren(prefix, wiki);
    kids.sort();
    return kids.length ? kids[0] : null;
};
```

After a server restart, {{{ [[docs/_first/intro]] }}} from any source
resolves to the alphabetically first immediate child under `docs/`.

''The contract:''

* `exports.name` — the full segment string, //including// the leading
  underscore. This is what appears in the user's reference.
* `exports.resolve(prefix, wiki)` — `prefix` is the `/`-joined segments
  before the pseudo (empty string if pseudo is at position 0). Return
  the replacement segment (non-empty string) or `null` to fail
  expansion.

''Utilities available from `resolver.js`:''

* `listImmediateChildren(prefix, wiki)` — returns the set of immediate
  child segments under `<prefix>/`, across regular and shadow tiddlers.
* `splitPath(title)` — splits a title into path segments, treating
  `$:/…` titles as a single segment.

Results are cached per `(pseudoName, prefix, wiki)` triple; the cache is
invalidated on wiki change events whose title touches the prefix.

!! How link rendering works

The wikirule converts {{{ [[REF]] }}} into:

```
<$let __nsref__="REF">
  <$link to={{{ [<__nsref__>ns-resolve<currentTiddler>] }}}
         class={{{ [<__nsref__>ns-resolve-class<currentTiddler>] }}}>
    text
  </$link>
</$let>
```

Resolution happens at render time via the `ns-resolve` filter operator,
which reads the `ns-context` variable (set by pragma or widget) with
fallback to the `context` field of `currentTiddler`. Because the
`currentTiddler` is the rendering context, references resolve correctly
under transclusion.

!! Backlinks & tooling

The plugin maintains its own backlinks index, built from a per-tiddler
scan that resolves every reference with the source tiddler's context.
Exposed as:

* Filter operator `[<title>ns-backlinks[]]` — sources that reference
  `<title>`.
* Filter operator `[<title>ns-forwardlinks[]]` — targets referenced by
  `<title>`.
* ''NS'' tab in the tiddler info sidebar — lists both, plus a
  ''Pin `_latest`'' button for tiddlers whose `\context` pragma contains
  drifting pseudos.

A ''settings'' plugin tab lists all registered pseudos, aliases, and
mount points for diagnostics.

!! Relink integration

When the [[relink plugin|https://github.com/flibbles/tw5-relink]] is
installed, renaming a tiddler automatically updates:

* `\context <prefix>` pragmas where the prefix matches the old title.
* `<$context prefix="...">` widget attributes.

Combined with the ''relink-titles'' companion plugin (which cascades
renames to child tiddlers), renaming a parent like `OWASP/ASVS` to
`OWASP/Standards` also updates `\context OWASP/ASVS/4.0.3` →
`\context OWASP/Standards/4.0.3` (because relink-titles renames the
child tiddler first, then relink updates the reference).

!! Deferred

* Namespace-aware transclusion `{{REF}}` — would require a full core
  transcludeinline replacement to cover template / field / index forms.
* Editor autocomplete for namespace-aware refs.

!! Current limitations

* Core TiddlyWiki's backlink index and the `relink` plugin only see the
  literal `[[REF]]` string, not the resolved target. The plugin ships
  its own backlinks index (see above) that resolves correctly.
* When a tiddler with a `context:` field is transcluded from a scope
  that set `ns-context` (via pragma or widget), the outer scope wins.
  Workaround: add `\context <same-value>` at the top of the inner
  tiddler if you need it to "own" its context regardless.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/realms
description: Toggle visibility of tiddler groups (realms) via TiddlyWiki filters
name: realms
author: rimir
readme:
! realms

Toggle visibility of tiddler groups from the browser using TiddlyWiki filter expressions. Uses a subtract model — all tiddlers are visible by default, ticking a realm hides its matching tiddlers.

!! Key features

* ''Filter-based realms'' — each realm defines a TiddlyWiki filter in `realms.json`
* ''Subtract model'' — all visible by default, tick to hide matching tiddlers
* ''Per-request filtering'' — server keeps all tiddlers in memory, filtering at the HTTP layer
* ''Settings UI'' — toggle realms on/off in ControlPanel with visual feedback
* ''No restart needed'' — `realms.json` is re-read per request

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Define realms in `realms.json` (next to `tiddlywiki.info`):

```
{ "work": { "filter": "[tag[Work]]", "description": "Work tiddlers", "active": false } }
```

Toggle visibility in ControlPanel > Settings > realms.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/runner
description: Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine via HTTP route
author: rimir
name: runner
list: readme documentation settings showcase changelog license
readme:
! runner

Execute whitelisted commands on the host machine via an HTTP route and trigger them from the browser with an action widget.

!! Key features

* ''Whitelist security'' — only commands defined in `runner-actions.json` can be executed
* ''Parameterized commands'' — `{{placeholder}}` templates substituted from query params
* ''Action widget'' — `<$api.run action="name" param="value"/>` for use inside `<$button>`
* ''HTTP route'' — `GET /api/run?action=<name>` for direct invocation

!! Prerequisites

* Commands must be configured in `runner-actions.json` (next to `tiddlywiki.info`)

!! Quick start

Add an action entry to `runner-actions.json`:

```
{ "myaction": { "command": "echo hello", "description": "Test" } }
```

Use in any tiddler:

```
<$button><$api.run action="myaction"/>Run</$button>
```

title: $:/plugins/rimir/scattered-binaries
description: Auto-discover and serve binary files from subdirectories via _canonical_uri
name: scattered binaries
author: rimir
readme:
! scattered binaries

Auto-discover binary files (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, etc.) scattered across subdirectories and serve them via `_canonical_uri` tiddlers — no manual `tiddlywiki.files` needed per directory.

!! Features

* ''Profile-based discovery'' — configure which directories to scan via JSON profiles
* ''Boot cleanup'' — removes duplicate tiddlers created by TW's boot scanner for binary files
* ''HTTP route'' — serves files via configurable route prefix with path traversal protection
* ''Meta-sidecar aware'' — skips files that have `.meta` sidecars (defers to TW native handling)
* ''Extension filtering'' — only known binary extensions are served (blocks `.env`, `.json`, etc.)

!! Optional integration

When ''file-upload'' is also installed:

* Discovered locations are automatically registered with the location registry
* Deleting/renaming a scattered-binary tiddler cascades to derived artifacts (extractions, summaries, etc.)
* Locations can be toggled writable via file-upload's settings UI

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki 5.3.0+ (Node.js server edition)

!! Quick Start

# Configure profiles in plugin settings (default profile scans `tiddlers/tender/#NNN/documents/`)
# Restart TiddlyWiki — binary files are auto-discovered and registered as `_canonical_uri` tiddlers
# Access files via the configured route prefix (e.g., `/tender-docs/%23512/filename.pdf`)

title: $:/plugins/rimir/statewrap
description: Channel-based state management with reactive rules for decoupled, reusable views
name: statewrap
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings showcase changelog license
readme:
! Statewrap

Channel-based state management for TiddlyWiki. Define named state channels in a container, and let child views read/write them by name — without knowing the underlying state tiddler paths.

!! Features

* ''Named channels'' — views reference logical names like `selected-project`, not `$:/state/...` paths
* ''Reactive rules'' — declarative wikitext actions fire when a channel changes (e.g., "when project changes, clear task selection")
* ''Instance isolation'' — each `<$statewrap>` gets its own state namespace via `instid` or TW's qualify mechanism
* ''Cascading rules'' — a rule's writes trigger matching downstream rules (e.g. section → entities → details), bounded by a depth cap (10) to prevent infinite loops. Suppress with `no-rules="yes"` on `<$action-statewrap-set>`.
* ''Filter operators'' — `[statewrap-get[channel]]` reads value, `[statewrap-ref[channel]]` returns state tiddler path, `[<instid>statewrap-lookup[channel]]` reads a channel's value without needing a parent `<$statewrap>` scope (for use in `\function` bodies, detached rendering, and introspection tools)

!! Quick start

```
<$statewrap channels="selected-project selected-task detail-tab"
            default-detail-tab="overview" instid="demo">
  <$statewrap-rule when="selected-project">
    <$action-statewrap-set channel="selected-task" value=""/>
    <$action-statewrap-set channel="detail-tab" value="overview"/>
  </$statewrap-rule>

  <%if [statewrap-get[selected-project]!is[blank]]%>
    Project: <$text text={{{[statewrap-get[selected-project]]}}}/>
  <%endif%>
</$statewrap>
```

!! API

|!Element |!Name |!Purpose |
|Container widget |`<$statewrap>` |Defines channels, defaults; establishes context |
|Rule widget |`<$statewrap-rule>` |Declares reactive wikitext actions for a channel change |
|Action widget |`<$action-statewrap-set>` |Write to a channel by name |
|Filter operator |`[statewrap-get[channel]]` |Read a channel's current value (requires ancestor `<$statewrap>`) |
|Filter operator |`[statewrap-ref[channel]]` |Get a channel's state tiddler path (requires ancestor `<$statewrap>`) |
|Filter operator |`[<instid>statewrap-lookup[channel]]` |Tree-independent channel read — takes the instid as filter input, so it works outside statewrap scope |

!! Prerequisites

TiddlyWiki 5.3.0 or later.

title: $:/plugins/rimir/theme
description: Shared visual theme for rimir plugins
name: theme
author: rimir
readme:
! theme Shared visual CSS classes (prefix `rr-`) used across all rimir plugins. Provides consistent styling for tabs, panels, tables, callouts, notices, badges, forms, status messages, and the settings hub layout. !! Key features * ''Tabs'' — `rr-tabs`, `rr-tab`, `rr-tab-active` for horizontal tab bars * ''Callouts'' — four types (info, warning, tip, danger) via reusable template * ''Notices'' — alert banners (warning, info, danger, success) * ''Badges'' — circular and rectangular pill badges * ''Settings hub layout'' — sidebar + content panel classes for core-hook * ''Utilities'' — muted, mono, nowrap, small helper classes !! Prerequisites No external prerequisites. !! Quick start Install plugin. Use `rr-` prefixed CSS classes in your tiddlers. For callouts: ``` <$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/theme/callout" type="info"> <$fill $name="body">Your message ``` !! Class reference !!! Tabs |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-tabs` |Flex container for tab bar | |`rr-tab` |Individual tab button | |`rr-tab-active` |Active tab (add alongside `rr-tab`) | ```
<$button class="rr-tab rr-tab-active">Tab 1 <$button class="rr-tab">Tab 2
``` !!! Panels |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-panel` |Bordered container with background | |`rr-panel-body` |Padded inner content area | !!! Tables |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-table` |Full-width collapsed table | |`rr-row-error` |Red background row (on ``) | |`rr-row-warn` |Yellow background row (on ``) | !!! Callouts Use the template `$:/plugins/rimir/theme/callout` with parameters `type` (`info`/`warning`/`tip`/`danger`) and optional `label`. |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-callout` |Base callout box | |`rr-callout-info` |Blue left border | |`rr-callout-warning` |Amber left border | |`rr-callout-tip` |Green left border | |`rr-callout-danger` |Red left border | |`rr-callout-label` |Bold uppercase label | !!! Notices Use the template `$:/plugins/rimir/theme/notice` with parameter `type` (`warning`/`info`/`danger`/`success`). |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-notice` |Base notice banner | |`rr-notice-warning` |Yellow background | |`rr-notice-info` |Light blue background | |`rr-notice-danger` |Light red background | |`rr-notice-success` |Light green background | !!! Status Messages |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-status-loading` |Muted italic loading text | |`rr-status-error` |Red alert box | |`rr-status-success` |Green success box | |`rr-status-info` |Muted info text | !!! Badges |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-badge-circle` |16px round badge | |`rr-badge-added` |Green circle (use with `rr-badge-circle`) | |`rr-badge-removed` |Red circle (use with `rr-badge-circle`) | |`rr-badge-rect` |Rectangular pill badge | !!! Forms |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-input-row` |Flex row with gap | |`rr-input` |Styled text input | !!! Link Buttons |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-link-btn` |Primary-colored link-style button | |`rr-link-btn-danger` |Alert-colored variant (add alongside `rr-link-btn`) | !!! Settings Hub |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-settings-hub` |Flex container for sidebar + content layout | |`rr-settings-sidebar` |Left sidebar with plugin list | |`rr-settings-plugin-item` |Sidebar button for each plugin | |`rr-settings-plugin-active` |Active state highlight (accent border) | |`rr-settings-plugin-name` |Plugin name text (truncated) | |`rr-settings-plugin-version` |Version badge | |`rr-settings-content` |Right content area | |`rr-settings-header` |Plugin header with icon + name + description | |`rr-settings-header-name` |Plugin name in header | |`rr-settings-header-desc` |Plugin description in header | |`rr-settings-info-card` |Dashed info card for plugins without settings | !!! Utilities |!Class |!Purpose | |`rr-muted` |Muted foreground color | |`rr-mono` |Monospace font | |`rr-nowrap` |No text wrapping | |`rr-small` |0.85em font size |

title: $:/plugins/rimir/typed
description: Type system engine for TiddlyWiki — schema definitions via tiddler fields
name: typed
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings validator spawner changelog license
readme:
! typed

Type system engine for TiddlyWiki. Define schemas via tiddler fields, validate instances, and create new typed tiddlers through a spawner UI.

!! Key features

* ''Type definitions'' — define schemas with `rrt.type` and `rrt.fields` on tiddler fields
* ''Field definitions'' — validation rules, typed editors, calculated fields
* ''Validator'' — UI to check all instances against their type schemas (see Validator tab)
* ''Spawner'' — UI to create new typed tiddler instances (see Spawner tab)
* ''Field editor'' — type-aware input controls for editing typed fields
* ''Inheritance'' — parent types for shared field definitions
* ''Custom filters'' — `rrfunc` filter functions for type system queries
* ''Inline field bar'' — compact editable field display for embedding in app views
* ''Field summary'' — read-only compact table of all typed fields with formatted values
* ''Filter panel'' — auto-generated filter UI from field definitions (text search, enum pills, reference pills)
* ''Filter summary'' — compact button with active filter count badge, opens filter panel in popup
* ''`rrt-filter` operator'' — JS filter operator for generic type-aware list filtering

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Define a type tiddler with `rrt.type` and field definitions. Use the Spawner tab to create instances. Use the Validator tab to check for schema violations across all typed tiddlers.
